title: What is an aging face supposed to look like?
description: A few months ago, while engaging in one of my more recent pastimes (or compulsions), I verbalized a fear I’d long kept buried, perhaps out of shame or denial or some combination of both. First, the compulsory ritual: Before bed, with the precision of a brain surgeon, I arrange a layer of stickers on my […]
hook: She performs skincare rituals like surgery to stop her face from betraying her age—a meditation on female visibility and invisible labor.

title: A Deaf Manifesto on Motherhood
description:
At age 12, Sara Nović went deaf, but not all at once. First, she lost the wind, then the dripping of a leaky faucet, and then certain consonants. She writes in her memoir, “What is a mother tongue, and how do you get one? What if your mother has no tongue? What if you have […]

The post A Deaf Manifesto on Motherhood appeared first on Electric Literature.
hook: A deaf woman writes about losing sound gradually, then motherhood—how do you pass on a language you're losing yourself?

title: Rebecca Lehmann on Anne Boleyn, Trump, and Treason
description: Writer Rebecca Lehmann joins co-hosts Whitney Terrell and V.V. Ganeshananthan to discuss her debut novel, The Beheading Game, a work of speculative historical fiction in which King Henry VIII’s second wife, the beheaded Queen of England Anne Boleyn, comes back
hook: Anne Boleyn comes back to life in speculative fiction to confront Trump-era treason. History as a mirror for now.

title: Teenage Sex and Death at Camp Miasma – first-look review
description: Jane Schoenbrun's third feature is a smart, sexy ode to the teen slasher, starring Hannah Einbinder and Gillian Anderson.
hook: Jane Schoenbrun makes a teen slasher that's actually smart—sexy, violent, and genuinely about something beneath the genre.

title: Tabula Raza
description: What comes after Asian American literature?
hook: The Baffler asks what happens to Asian American literature once the category itself becomes exhausted and irrelevant.
